page: Amazon Web Services i4i.32xlarge is a storage-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores using the x86_64 architecture, specifically utilizing Intel Xeon 8375C processors running at 3.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, which provides 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. For high-speed local storage, the instance includes 30,000 GB of NVMe SSD capacity. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 75 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the i4i.32xlarge demonstrates strong multi-core performance, placing in the top 25% for multi-core CPU stress tests and Geekbench multi-core scores. Its cryptographic and extended instruction capabilities are highly rated, achieving top-tier status in Passmark CPU encryption and extended instruction tests. Memory bandwidth is also robust, with uncached read and write speeds ranking in the top 25%. Database performance is highlighted by strong results in Passmark database operations and Redis SET operations, which reach 104.77 million operations per second. However, single-core performance and large-scale LLM text generation represent relative performance trade-offs, with the latter falling into the bottom 25%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the instance offers a high density of local NVMe SSD storage and memory relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for data-intensive deployments without requiring external storage attachments. This hardware profile is highly suited for large-scale transactional databases, NoSQL databases, data warehousing, and high-throughput web serving workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services i4i.32xlarge is a storage-optimized instance designed for data-intensive workloads. Powered by the Nitro hypervisor, it features 128 vCPUs (64 physical cores) on an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz, alongside 1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 30,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps. Performance benchmarks indicate top-tier capabilities in cryptographic and extended instruction processing, alongside strong multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth metrics. Database operations and Redis performance are highly efficient, though single-core execution and large-scale LLM text generation show lower relative performance. This instance is optimized for high-throughput databases, large-scale data warehousing, and web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i4i.32xlarge is a storage-optimized instance featuring 128 vCPUs, 1024 GB DDR4 RAM, 30,000 GB NVMe SSD storage, and 75 Gbps network bandwidth for database and high-throughput workloads.

tagline: A storage-optimized platform featuring massive local NVMe SSD capacity, high memory bandwidth, and strong multi-core performance for demanding database workloads.

1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory paired with 30,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth utilizing the Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier performance in cryptographic operations and extended CPU instructions
Strong multi-core processing and high-throughput database execution capabilities
Best suited for large-scale transactional databases, NoSQL systems, and data warehousing
